quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hungary is home to over 1,500 natural thermal springs — more than any other country in Europe! The tradition of thermal bathing dates back to Roman times, and many Hungarians still visit thermal baths regularly for relaxation and health. Budapest alone has more than 100 thermal springs feeding its famous bathhouses.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Hummingbirds
short_read.teaser: These tiny jewels of the bird world have hearts that beat 1,200 times per minute and can fly backwards! Discover the amazing adaptations that make hummingbirds some of nature's most extraordinary creatures.

medium_read.title: The Women Who Mapped the Ocean Floor
medium_read.teaser: Marie Tharp was a geologist who created the first detailed map of the ocean floor in the 1950s, revealing underwater mountains and the Mid-Atlantic Ridge. Her groundbreaking work helped prove the theory of continental drift and changed our understanding of Earth forever.

long_read.title: The Danube: Europe's Amazon
long_read.teaser: The Danube River flows through ten countries and four capital cities, including Budapest, making it one of the world's most international rivers. This journey explores the river's rich history, diverse wildlife, and the cultures that have thrived along its banks for thousands of years.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season
port_angeles.description: July is peak harbor seal pupping season in the Strait of Juan de Fuca! Mother seals give birth to their pups on rocky shores and beaches around Port Angeles. You might spot these adorable families at Ediz Hook or along the waterfront — remember to observe from a distance to keep them safe and comfortable.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's wings can beat up to ___ times per second.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! Those tiny wings are incredibly fast — so fast we can't even see them move!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! Hummingbird wings beat about 80 times per second — that's what creates their distinctive humming sound.
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Hummingbird wings actually beat about 80 times per second! That's why they make that wonderful humming sound we hear.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: The ruby-throated hummingbird, which visits the Pacific Northwest in summer, can beat its wings 53 times per second during normal flight, but up to 200 times per second during courtship dives!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful, Ildiko! Pillangó is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound lovely? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language unrelated to most European languages — it's part of the Finno-Ugric family. Many Hungarian words are delightfully descriptive, like 'pillangó' which comes from a word meaning 'to flutter.'

puzzle_3.question: What ocean mammal can hold its breath for up to two hours?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Elephant seals are incredible divers — they can dive nearly a mile deep and hold their breath for up to two hours while hunting for fish and squid!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the elephant seal! These amazing creatures can hold their breath for up to two hours and dive nearly a mile deep. Harbor seals, which you might see in Port Angeles, can hold their breath for about 30 minutes.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Elephant seals slow their heart rate to just a few beats per minute while diving, conserving oxygen. Some elephant seals migrate past the Olympic Peninsula on their way between breeding grounds in California and feeding areas in Alaska.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
